title: calophyllum pinetorum
intro: Calophyllum pinetorum, also known as the red sap tree, is a species of evergreen tree in the family Calophyllaceae. The tree is native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with the sap and bark of the tree being used to treat various health issues. The sap of the Calophyllum pinetorum contains limonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, providing health benefits such as wound healing and anti-infection properties. The herbal preparations of the Calophyllum pinetorum can be made into ointments or tinctures, which can be applied topically to treat skin conditions and wounds, or taken orally to aid in digestion and immune system support.
